# CloseInATrust

> CloseInATrust (closeinatrust.com) helps U.S. homeowners and real-estate investors get mortgages that close with a trust or LLC on title — purchases and refinances vested in revocable living trusts, irrevocable trusts, and LLCs. It matches borrowers with licensed specialists whose lenders handle entity vesting directly, including DSCR loans that qualify on the property's rent. CloseInATrust is an AdaptLend service (adaptlend.com) and is not a lender.

## Key facts

- A trust can get a mortgage: many lenders close loans vested in revocable living trusts; specialist lenders handle irrevocable trusts. The trustee signs and the estate plan stays intact.
- An LLC can get a mortgage: DSCR and investor programs routinely close with the LLC on title — most DSCR lenders prefer entity vesting. The rent qualifies the loan, not personal income.
- Banks that demand you "take the house out of the trust" are working around their own processing limits, not a law — the deed-out/deed-back shuffle is avoidable with the right lender.
- For a primary residence in a revocable trust, the Garn-St Germain Act generally protects transferring the home into the trust AFTER closing from due-on-sale clauses — an honest alternative when it fits. It does not apply to LLCs, irrevocable trusts, or investment property.
- Entity-vested investor loans generally price somewhat above owner-occupied conventional rates.
- Filling out the CloseInATrust form does not affect credit; a credit check only happens if the borrower moves forward with a specialist.

## How it works

1. Answer a few plain-English questions (about two minutes, no credit check, no login).
2. CloseInATrust matches the borrower with a licensed specialist whose lenders close trust/LLC vestings regularly.
3. The specialist contacts the borrower within one business day.

- [Free DSCR calculator](https://lumolend.com/tools/dscr-calculator/) — LumoLend
- [LumoLend](https://lumolend.com/) — price purchase, refinance, and HELOC scenarios with live indicative rates; free calculators and 40+ mortgage guides
